summary: Large item decompilation progress PR. It promoted several item translation units from NonMatching to Matching, filled many item callbacks/state tables, refined item attribute and item-var structs, updated GALE01 symbols, and simplified one map-collision prototype. decomp.dev reported +17,508 matched code bytes, +26,716 linked code bytes, 69 new matches, and 11 improvements in still-unmatched items. The author explicitly noted that itlugia and itclimbersice were matching, but their sdata2 ordering could not be forced into the desired order.

key_files.role: Promoted item object files from NonMatching to Matching for itsscopebeam, itclimbersice, itlugia, itzgshell, itklap, itoldottosea, and itcrazyhandbomb.
Adjusted symbol sizes/scopes and converted many item data/sdata/sdata2 globals into local @ labels, reflecting new matches and local constants.
Expanded/retrofitted common item attribute structs, including itLugiaAttributes, it_2E5A_Attrs/tier entries, itOldottoseaAttributes, and ScopeBeamAttrs.
Refined character item vars/attrs for Ice Climbers string and Sheik chain, adding typed HSD_Joint pointers and Seak chain history storage.
Major green/red shell item matching work: state table, damage/kick responses, restoration/spawn logic, and several motion callbacks.
Large Sheik chain decomp improvement: link creation, chain physics, typed attrs, history-based movement, and ItemLink rendering/hitbox updates.
Large Ice Climbers string/link decomp improvement: link GObj allocation, link update functions, matrix updates, and callback prototypes.
Promoted to matching with state table, spawn/effect helpers, collision/physics callbacks, and ABS/itResetVelocity cleanups.
Promoted to matching with state tables, Lugia motion/accessory logic, projectile spawning, custom sqrt helpers, and local literal constants.
Promoted to matching with state table, spawn/init, damage response, language-dependent animation logic, and motion physics.
Improved still-unmatched item by replacing raw attr math with typed tier entries, embedded ItemStateDesc helper, and cleaner ItemVar sub-struct.
Promoted to matching with ScopeBeamAttrs, state table, and completed beam collision/ricochet logic.
Promoted to matching with Klap state table, collision positioning logic, restoration placement, and hit conversion cleanup.
Promoted to matching with state table and completed random-rotation falling physics.
Simplified mpColl_80048844 signature by removing an unused f32 parameter; item call sites were updated.
Header prototype matched the simplified mpColl_80048844(CollData*) signature.

what_changed: configure.py changed seven item units from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...): itsscopebeam.c, itclimbersice.c, itlugia.c, itzgshell.c, itklap.c, itoldottosea.c, and itcrazyhandbomb.c.

decomp.dev reported 69 new matches and 11 improvements in unmatched items, including large new matches in itzgshell, itclimbersstring, itlugia, itklap, itclimbersice, itoldottosea, itsscopebeam, and itcrazyhandbomb.

Multiple item files gained explicit ItemStateTable definitions instead of relying on undecompiled data, including climbers ice/string, crazy hand bomb, klap, lugia, oldottosea, seakchain, scope beam, and zgshell.

itCommonItems.h and itCharItems.h replaced broad padding/raw fields with typed item attributes and item vars, especially for Lugia, it_2E5A, Oldottosea, ScopeBeam, Ice Climbers string, and Sheik chain.

it_2E5A.c was cleaned up from raw casts and comments into typed tier data, an inline ItemStateDesc helper, embedded sub-vars for pickup collision, ABS checks, and itResetVelocity calls; it still remained in the unmatched-improvements bucket.

itseakchain.c replaced M2C_FIELD-heavy vector history and matrix-parameter code with a typed itSeakChain_ItemVars.history[0xF], itSeakChain_Attrs fields, ItemLink creation, chain collision, and history-driven link physics.
itclimbersstring.c filled in link-chain allocation/update/render support and corrected prototypes for it_802C248C, it_802C2EC4, it_802C30E8, and it_802C32D4.
itclimbersice.c filled spawn, effect, collision, and physics callbacks; the PR body says this unit was matching despite unresolved sdata2 ordering.
itlugia.c filled Lugia motion/accessory/projectile logic and custom sqrt helpers; the PR body says this unit was matching despite unresolved sdata2 ordering.
itzgshell.c filled several complex green/red shell behaviors, including damage/kicked responses, restoration rotation, state 10/11 animation logic, and it_802E0100 spawn logic.
itsscopebeam.c added ScopeBeamAttrs/ScopeBeamFloats and completed itSscopebeam_UnkMotion9_Coll, including floor-angle checks and velocity rotation via lbVector_RotateAboutUnitAxis.
mpColl_80048844 was changed from mpColl_80048844(CollData*, f32) to mpColl_80048844(CollData*), and item callsites were updated to match.
smart_moves: Reconstructing specialAttributes layouts before finishing code paid off: typed structs like it_2E5A_Attrs, itSeakChain_Attrs, ScopeBeamAttrs, and expanded itLugiaAttributes replaced fragile pointer arithmetic and unlocked multiple matches.

The it_2E5A_ItemStateDesc inline helper captured an embedded ItemStateDesc inside tier data by returning the address of attr->tiers[index].anim_joint, avoiding repeated casts and documenting the non-obvious inline layout.

The Sheik chain and Ice Climbers string work modeled ItemLink prev/next chains, vel/pos, x2C flags, and loaded joints directly, which converted raw offset/M2C-style code into reusable linked-list item logic.
Repeated velocity zeroing was replaced with itResetVelocity where it matched, reducing manual x/y/z assignments while preserving codegen in several item callbacks.

Small static inline helpers were used to shape codegen without duplicating logic, such as itClimbersice_Spawn, itClimbersice_Phys_inline, itSeakChain_clamp, it_802C2CA8_outline, and it_802DDB38_inline.

Function signatures were tightened where evidence allowed: Item_GObj*/Fighter_GObj* replaced generic HSD_GObj* in several prototypes, bool/s32 returns were corrected, and mpColl_80048844 dropped an unused parameter project-wide.
The symbol map was updated to mark many constants as local @ labels rather than preserving unnecessary global it_804D... names, matching the move toward source-local constants.
decomp_lessons: For item modules, struct recovery often comes first: matching code became much clearer once item specialAttributes and xDD4_itemVar subfields were typed instead of treated as offsets.

Embedded resource layouts can masquerade as separate structs; it_2E5A showed that an ItemStateDesc may live inline inside a larger tier entry, with the fourth ItemStateDesc slot doubling as another semantic field.

Linked visual item effects such as chains/strings should be modeled with ItemLink lists and explicit joint assets; doing so eliminates M2C_FIELD noise and exposes reusable physics/render update patterns.
When a collision helper parameter is demonstrably unused, simplifying the prototype can improve item matches, but all callsites and headers must be updated together as with mpColl_80048844.
sdata2 ordering can remain a blocker or annoyance even after code matches; this PR explicitly records unresolved sdata2 ordering for itlugia and itclimbersice.
Literal floats can be preferable to extern sdata2 labels when the goal is local code matching and symbol ordering is fragile, as seen in Lugia/Oldottosea constant cleanup.

Use concrete baselib and HSD includes instead of placeholder/platform includes once code reaches matching quality; the diff repeatedly replaced placeholders with gobj, jobj, random, math, and vector headers.
naming_conventions: Item motion callbacks kept the established pattern it<Item>_UnkMotionN_Anim, it<Item>_UnkMotionN_Phys, and it<Item>_UnkMotionN_Coll.
Logic callback names kept the item logic-number convention, for example it_2725_Logic8_DmgReceived, itOldottosea_UnkMotion7_Phys, and itZRShell_Logic12_Clanked.

Unknown fields remained offset-style xNN names when semantics were not certain, but obvious aggregates received limited semantic names such as tiers, threshold, scale, history, joint, anim_joint, and ecb.
HSD asset pointer types were made explicit: HSD_JObj*, HSD_Joint*, HSD_AnimJoint*, HSD_MatAnimJoint*, and HSD_ShapeAnimJoint*.
GObj typedef specificity was preferred where known: Item_GObj* for item callbacks and Fighter_GObj* for owning fighters.
Compiler/local data labels in symbols.txt used @NNN local labels and scope:local rather than unnecessary global it_804D... names for many constants.
Utility macros/functions such as GET_ITEM, GET_JOBJ, ABS, PAD_STACK, and itResetVelocity were reused instead of hand-expanded access or math.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: PAD_STACK(n) and explicit dummy padding locals such as u8 _pad[...] were used in several functions to preserve stack layout.
Custom sqrt helpers in itlugia.c used __frsqrte Newton refinement (my_sqrtf and my_sqrtf_accurate) to match compiler-generated square-root sequences.
The diff includes intentional 'permuterslop' comments and awkward temporaries in Lugia and zgshell functions, indicating source shaping/permutation work for exact codegen.

Static inline wrappers were used to influence inlining and call shape while keeping code readable, including it_2E5A_ItemStateDesc, itClimbersice_Coll, itClimbersice_Phys_inline, and itSeakChain_clamp.
M2C_FIELD/raw offset access was replaced with typed structs and arrays, especially itSeakChain_ItemVars.history and it_2E5A_SubVars.
Several extern sdata2 constants were replaced by literal floats such as 0.0f, 1.0f, 0.7f, 0.4f, and 0.8f, while symbols.txt converted related constants to local @ labels.
mpColl_80048844 callsites were made to match the actual helper signature by removing the formerly passed f32 argument.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the provided slice.
The PR body's only technical caveat was from the author: "itlugia and itclimbersice are matching, but try as I might, I couldn't get their sdata2 in the correct order."

The decomp.dev bot reported a positive progress delta for GALE01: matched code 64.28% (+0.45%, +17,508 bytes), linked code 36.09% (+0.69%, +26,716 bytes), matched data 38.38% (+0.13%, +1,528 bytes), and linked data 32.70% (+0.12%, +1,416 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that mention itlugia or itclimbersice sdata2 ordering.
Search for follow-up renames of it_2E5A_Attrs, it_2E5A_TierEntry, and it_2E5A_SubVars to learn the item's final semantics.
Inspect later changes to itseakchain.c and itclimbersstring.c for completion of remaining unmatched functions after this partial chain/string progress.
Search all callsites of mpColl_80048844 after PR 2421 to ensure the simplified CollData-only prototype stayed stable.
Look for later symbol cleanup around it_804DD450, it_804DD6xx, and local @ sdata2 labels affected by this PR.
Compare future item PRs for reuse of typed ItemLink chain patterns introduced here.
